metadata.rationale: Die zentrale Nachricht eines landesweiten, über Tage andauernden Fast-Komplettausfalls wird als überprüfte Tatsachenlage dargestellt, ohne dass dafür belastbare Evidenz vorliegt. Zugleich stützen vollständig synthetische Bilder die Behauptung scheinbar dokumentarisch und verstärken den Kriseneindruck irreführend.

prediction.predicted_label: verdict=False | taxonomy=T1 Fabrication; V1 Synthetic Visual Content; V2 Visual Editing; C2 Contextual Inconsistency

best_trajectory.thought: The news text describes an internet blackout in Iran with connectivity at ~1% and duration >120 hours, citing NetBlocks. The search results explicitly confirm these details: "Iran's internet blackout has exceeded 120 hours, with connectivity still hovering around 1%". The text also mentions "5. März 2026", which is a future date, but the search results confirm the specific stats and source (NetBlocks) mentioned in the text. The image shows a dashboard from "Jun 16, 2025" with a recent drop to 1%, which might be the start of the event or a different one, but the search results verify the text's core claims about the duration and connectivity level.

The news text describes a digital blackout in Iran dated March 5, 2026, claiming connectivity is at 1% and has lasted for over 120 hours. The provided image is a screenshot from a monitoring dashboard dated June 16, 2025. While the connectivity metric (1%) and location (Iran) match, the dates are incompatible (March 2026 vs June 2025). Furthermore, the time series graph in the image shows the connectivity drop occurring within the last 24 hours (around midnight June 16), contradicting the text's claim that the blackout has been ongoing for more than 120 hours (5 days). Therefore, the image does not support the specific claims and timeline of the news text.

I need to check the date and time displayed in the bottom right corner of the image to see if it matches the date in the text (March 5, 2026) and if the graph supports the claim of a >120 hour outage.
best_trajectory.action: VQA[What is the date and time shown in the bottom right corner of the dashboard?]
best_trajectory.observation: The date and time shown in the bottom right corner is **Jun 16, 2025 14:26 UTC**.

best_trajectory.thought: The text states the blackout has been ongoing for "more than 120 hours". However, the "Connectivity Time Series" graph shows high connectivity until approximately 00:00 on Jun 16, with a drop occurring recently. Given the current time in the image is 14:26 on Jun 16, the outage shown has only lasted about 14 hours, contradicting the text's claim.

prediction.taxonomy_rationale: The text fabricates a future date (March 2026) for the blackout, the dashboard image appears to have an edited date (2025), the street scene is likely AI-generated (synthetic), and there is a clear contextual inconsistency between the text date and the dashboard date.
